name: Puig des Tossals Verds — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park
name_original: Puig des Tossals Verds — circular trailrunning por el Paraje Natural de la Serra de Tramuntana
region_intro: Jogging around Escorca offers diverse and dramatic landscapes within Mallorca's Serra de Tramuntana mountain range, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The region is characterized by towering peaks, lush valleys, and a varied terrain that includes artificial lakes, holm oak forests, and unspoiled coastal coves. This environment provides an exceptional setting for running, with well-established routes catering to various difficulty levels. Best jogging routes around Escorca The most popular jogging route is Puig des Tossals Verds — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging circular experience within the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Cúber Reservoir on the GR 221 – Biniaraix loop from Embassament de Cúber, a difficult 12.7 miles (20.4 km) path. This route forms a significant loop from the Cúber Reservoir, incorporating a section of the GR 221. Local runners also love the Lluc Sanctuary – Sanctuary of Lluc loop from Lluc Sanctuary, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the cultural site, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around Escorca is defined by the majestic Serra de Tramuntana mountains, serene reservoirs, and ancient holm oak forests. The extensive trail network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult runs, accommodating various ability levels. The routes in Escorca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 17 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Escorca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Escorca?
A: Escorca offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging long-distance runs within the stunning Serra de Tramuntana.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Escorca's running trails?
A: The terrain in Escorca is incredibly diverse, reflecting its location within the Serra de Tramuntana. You'll encounter rugged mountain paths, lush valleys, and areas around serene reservoirs. Some routes feature mossy holm oak forests, while others lead to unspoiled coastal coves. Expect a mix of challenging ascents and descents, typical of a mountainous UNESCO World Heritage site.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Escorca?
A: While many routes in Escorca are challenging, there are options suitable for families or those seeking an easier run. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6914099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Major – Cúber Reservoir loop from Embassament de Cúber&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6 km route that offers beautiful views without extreme difficulty, making it a good choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Escorca?
A: Many trails in Escorca are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check specific route regulations or signage for any restrictions.

Q: Are there many circular running routes in Escorca?
A: Yes, Escorca features several excellent circular running routes that allow you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000787982/puig-des-tossals-verds-circular-trailrunning-in-the-serra-de-tramuntana-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig des Tossals Verds — circular trailrunning in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.5-mile trail offering a comprehensive experience of the natural park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Escorca?
A: Escorca's routes are rich with natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can run past the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúber Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore areas near the majestic Puig Major, or discover the unique mossy holm oak forests around Lluc. The region is also home to impressive canyons like Torrent de Pareis and unspoiled coves such as Sa Calobra and Cala Tuent. Additionally, you might encounter historic structures like terraces and snow houses along the trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Escorca?
A: The running routes in Escorca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 17 reviews. Runners frequently praise the stunning and diverse landscapes, from towering peaks to serene reservoirs and ancient forests. The challenging yet rewarding nature of the trails, especially those within the Serra de Tramuntana, is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Escorca?
A: While Escorca is known for its challenging trails, there are a few easier options suitable for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6914099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Major – Cúber Reservoir loop from Embassament de Cúber&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that provides a taste of the region's beauty without requiring advanced trail running experience.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints along the running trails?
A: Many running routes in Escorca offer spectacular viewpoints. The area around Puig Major, Mallorca's highest peak, provides breathtaking panoramic vistas. Trails leading to Col de l'Ofre from Cúber reservoir offer fantastic views of the Sóller valley. Even moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6914099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Major – Cúber Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; provide scenic outlooks over the reservoir and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any caves or mountain passes I can explore near the running routes?
A: Yes, Escorca is surrounded by fascinating geological features. You can find several caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4015385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campanet Caves&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1854921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Migdia&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from nearby areas. The region is also home to significant mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/75857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Sa Batalla&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/232995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Femenia&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular for their challenging ascents and scenic beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Escorca?
A: The best time to go running in Escorca is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable. Winters offer cooler temperatures but some higher trails might be affected by snow or more challenging conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Sanctuary of Lluc?
A: Yes, the Sanctuary of Lluc is a significant cultural and spiritual site that serves as a focal point for several routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3964125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluc Sanctuary – Sanctuary of Lluc loop from Lluc Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.3-mile trail that leads through the beautiful area surrounding this historic site, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.
